key_insight: A Lean proof agent can improve itself by jointly evolving its proof workflow and the benchmark it trains against, while still requiring Lean-verified proof success as the only acceptance criterion.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical path to stronger theorem-proving systems: use a small trusted core with mutable prompts, tools, and workflow around a verifier-grounded loop.
method: A self-modifying Lean agent rewrites its workflow, prompts, tools, and proof-context representation inside a trusted runtime.
The benchmark coevolves via a mastery-throttled curriculum that unlocks harder tasks only after current tasks are mastered.
A single-anchor recalibration re-runs the champion on the updated benchmark to preserve score comparability as difficulty rises.
evidence_signal: Across 15 active generations, the best coevolving agent reached 45.1% held-out solve rate on miniF2F, versus 12.7% for the seed and 32.0% for the best fixed-benchmark baseline.
watch_item: Because the benchmark changes over time, careful calibration is needed to ensure improvements reflect real reasoning gains rather than curriculum or scoring artifacts.
paper_title: Self-Modifying Lean Proof Agents with Verifier-Grounded Benchmark Coevolution
